name: FUN_00402190
summary: Primary constructor for major game entity class, likely Seaman creature or similar complex object. Stores 9 parameters: param_9→+0x5c, param_2→+0x3d0, param_4→+0x5a0, param_3→+0x59c, param_5→+0x358/+0x364, param_6→+0x35c/+0x368, param_7→+0x360/+0x36c (lines 279-307). Switch on param_2 (lines 281-293): cases 0/3 set +0x54=0, case 1 sets +0x54=2, cases 2/4/5 set +0x54=1 (mode selector). Conditional on param_3 (lines 266-269): if 0, sets +0x39c=0 and +0x8f4=1. Conditional on param_8 (lines 308-313): if 1 sets +0xa08=1.0f (0x3f800000), else sets +0xa08=DAT_00458200. Copies default string from DAT_00465090 to +0x908 via inline strlen+memcpy (lines 314-336). Zeros control fields +0x8fc, +0xa0c through +0xa1c, +0xa2c, +0xa30 (lines 337-344).

name: FUN_004023a0
summary: Comprehensive destructor for large game entity (likely creature/Seaman object). Begins with safety check (infinite loop if offset 0x299==1 prevents destruction while in use), saves biological state (Grow/Hunger/timestamps) to database, methodically destroys 13+ subsystem objects via vtable calls, cleans up two linked lists at +0x16a, and conditionally calls Deletion() if flag at +0xf4 is set (also invalidates global reference at DAT_004690a0+0x540 = 0xffffffff). Uses SEH protection and local_4 state tracking for exception-safe cleanup.

name: FUN_00402670
summary: Core per-frame update function orchestrating all Seaman subsystems. Detects cursor waggling via FUN_004038f0, dispatches actions via FUN_004052f0, manages hunger decay, handles evolution by loading stage-specific PAK files (GM1_1.pak through HG2_6.pak) using DNA matrix at DAT_00458224 to select variant (1-6), updates game clock via FUN_0043f630/0043f640, normalizes direction vectors, computes bounding boxes, applies swimming motion offset when idle, and processes mail system (GetMailList→execute_speech_template→SetRelationSelectDB). Evolution triggers on growth level changes (0→1→3→5→8) with automatic model reload and database save.

name: FUN_004031f0
summary: Complete creature update pipeline executing every frame (30-60Hz) that orchestrates animation, AI, and rendering. First applies skeletal skinning transformations to mesh vertices via dual-pass FUN_0041bc60 (bone 0) and single-pass FUN_0041be40 (bone 6). Dispatches CHigyoBrain AI via FUN_00405610 with 12 parameters including delta time, objects, and position data, handling return codes: 2=breeding trigger (sets this+0x34c=1, zeros mate reference at this+0x5a4+0x350), 3=special event (zeros this+0xa10). Generates procedural swimming motion via FUN_00403510 when not paused (this+0x39c==0). Updates camera transforms via multiple vtable calls (+0x10, +0xec, +0xf0, +0x90) positioning creature with swimming offset. Checks breeding eligibility via FUN_00403440 when timer this+0xa0c exceeds threshold _DAT_00452668, setting breeding flag this+0xa10 on success. Accumulates frame time into breeding timer (line 317). Writes final breeding/mate status to output structure via puVar4 (lines 318-319).

name: FUN_00403620
summary: Complete creature frame processing function executing AI dispatch (with delta time conversion param_2 * DAT_00452664), handling state return codes (2=breeding flag, 5=special event), normalizing direction vector at this+0x370-0x378 via SQRT magnitude calculation, setting camera view transform with negated direction, applying growth-stage-dependent material colors from DAT_00458200 lookup table, conditionally scaling material properties when this+0x3b0 flag active, updating position transform, and copying state flags this+0x348/34c to output buffer puVar6. Includes one-time scene object initialization via FUN_00439e40 when this+0x8f8 is zero.

name: FUN_00403a80
summary: User tap interaction handler for Seaman creatures. Validates Seaman ID at param_1+0x5c is not -1, queries creature type via get_myself_db_wrapper, skips if type 3 (likely visitor Seaman). For player's Seaman, sets database variable 'SeamanPC\Control\TapFlag' to 1, then triggers speech using global context DAT_004689fc.

name: FUN_00404770
summary: Sets SeamanPC\Control\MessageFlag variable to 1 via FUN_004012c0 if DAT_00461390 != -1 and param_1+0x5c != -1, then calls FUN_00434320 with global pointer DAT_004689fc and param_1+0x5c. Notification system for message delivery.

name: FUN_00405610
summary: Primary entry point for CHigyoBrain behavioral dispatch system, routing execution based on growth stage. Switches on param_8[0x11] growth indicator (lines 76-147): **EGG stage** (0.0): If state is 0x36 (end action), triggers speech via FUN_00434320 with param_12, resets state to 0, clears timer and flags (lines 78-84). Calls FUN_004127d0 (egg-only animation handler) and returns 0 without entering main state machine (lines 85-86). **Advanced stages** (1/3/5/8 as IEEE 754 floats 1.4013e-45/4.2039e-45/7.00649e-45/1.12104e-44): If state is 0x36, branches on param_10 mode selector (lines 91-144). Normal mode (param_10==0): Sets state 5 (idle), randomizes timer to (rand()&1)+2 seconds * DAT_004526a8, calculates camera jitter position via FUN_0041aa30 with ranges 12/25/1/60, stores at this+0xe0/e4/e8 (lines 92-104). Special mode (param_10!=0): Sets flag this+0x1fc=1, copies param_11 string to this+0x20c buffer via inline memcpy, sets state 2, timer 0.7s, position to -(GetSystemMetrics(0)/2) * DAT_004526a8 for X with Y/Z=0 (lines 105-135). Both modes: Calls FUN_00414cd0 to update distance, sets flags this+0x18c=1.0f and this+0x1a8=1, calculates Euclidean distance to param_8 target, stores at this+400 (lines 136-143). Calls FUN_00407e60 main state machine dispatcher (lines 145-146). Returns dispatcher result or 0 for EGG (line 149).

name: FUN_00405870
summary: Time-based hunger progression implementing threshold trigger mechanism. Reads hunger level from 'SeamanPC\Bio\Hunger' variable via get_variable_index_wrapper (line 2268). Branches on hunger level: Level 1 (lines 2269-2274): multiplies param_1 (delta time) by global _DAT_004526ac rate, adds to this+500 accumulator (offset 500 = 0x1f4), checks if accumulator >= threshold _DAT_004526b0, returns early if below threshold. Levels 2-3 (lines 2276-2287): validates range 2 <= level <= 3 (returns if outside), multiplies param_1 by different rate _DAT_004526b4, accumulates to this+500, checks same threshold _DAT_004526b0. On threshold exceeded (both paths converge at line 2289): calls list_move_node_to_front(this, 0x20) to trigger event (line 2289), resets this+500 to zero (line 2290). Other hunger levels (<1 or >3) are no-ops (lines 2277-2281).

name: FUN_00407e60
summary: Core AI dispatcher implementing dual-layer state machine architecture for Seaman creature behavior. Entry logic: calls FUN_00427060 + FUN_0041c580 to check game context (returns 0xf for special mode), sets animation speed pair via FUN_0041a400 (0x3c449ba6 for special mode, 0x3ccccccd default - these are float bit patterns for ~0.012 and ~0.025), generates random timing values (5-10 range scaled by DAT_004526f8) via FUN_0041a420. First dispatch tier: switch on this+0xd4 (alternate state system) handles states 0x22-0x27 including roll recovery (0x22), camera descent (0x23), evade (0x24), attack lunge (0x25), and directional blends (0x26-0x27). Second dispatch tier: switch on this+0xa4 (main state system) handles 54 behavioral states from state 0 (EGG idle) to state 0x36 (end action sentinel). Critical states: 0x00=EGG idle, 0x01-0x02=click handlers, 0x03=input navigation with sound, 0x17=click menu (Phase 24 discovery), 0x1d=DNA inheritance (breeding), 0x1f=copulation, 0x24=feeding, 0x2a=sleep, 0x2d=mail reaction, 0x30=speech reaction, 0x33=UI widget tracking, 0x36=error/end marker. Error handling: displays MessageBox with 'bad_mActionStatus' or 'bad_actionstatus_eEndAction' string, forces state to 5 (fallback). Returns 0 for most states, non-zero codes for special events (code 4 from collision navigation, code 6 from breeding genetics). Updates physics throughout via state handler calls to FUN_004131e0/FUN_00413320.

name: FUN_00408f30
summary: Comprehensive state machine implementing the 'Return Mail' feature where Seaman is sent to another player via email. State 0: Analyzes input direction via FUN_00414a30 (returns 1-4), sets camera angles at this+0xd4/d8 (values 0x22-0x25), decodes growth stage from param_5[0x11] float bit pattern (matching animation system encoding), stores absolute animation ID at this+0x1b0 (3/0x10/0x24/0x40/0x54/0x68), plays animation via FUN_00414610, enables visibility. State 1: Runs animation timer using DAT_00459f98 lookup table, checks collision via FUN_00412c80, plays completion sound when reached. State 2: Retrieves email address via FUN_00401460, constructs database file path (hostdb/messengerdb/visitordb + _message.udb based on local_308 type 0/1/2), writes message via FUN_004013b0, launches email thread via FUN_00428980. State 3: Checks send status flags at +0x1b6/+0x1b7, deletes temporary .udb files (messengerdb_seaman.udb + messengerdb_user.udb OR visitordb_seaman.udb), sets completion state at this+0xa4 to 0x36. Returns status codes 0/1/2 indicating progress.

name: FUN_00409ec0
summary: Mail notification reaction handler implementing BIO speech trigger for incoming messages. State 0: Calls clear_speech_path_triple (FUN_004344e0) to zero three 256-byte buffers at local_30c/20c/10c. Uses manual strlen+memcpy loops to copy three string constants: module name from DAT_00459f30 (likely 'Ogilby' or similar), category 'Reaction_Mail' from s_Reaction_Mail_00459f20 (one of the 7 hardcoded BIO categories), and subcategory from DAT_00465090 (possibly empty or message type). Calls start_bio_speech_for_context (FUN_004342f0) with in_stack_00000014 context ID and local_30c triple pointer to trigger speech playback. This is the bridge between mail system events and creature vocal response - when player receives messages via seamail.tv system, Seaman comments on them using this speech path.

name: FUN_0040c9f0
summary: 3-state catch/capture reaction handler. State 0 stops any previous speech, copies DAT_00459f30 module path and 'reaction_catch' category to speech buffer (local_30c/20c/10c), triggers BIO speech via start_bio_speech_for_context, transitions to state 1. State 1 initializes identity matrix (local_33c), calls compose_triple_rodrigues_extract_axis with angles (-45°, 0°, 0°) to compute tilted direction vector, writes result to param_4+0x18/0x1c/0x20, sets param_4+0x68=20.0 and param_4+0x58=1 (likely speed/mode flags), selects growth-specific animation ID (8/0x17/0x2c/0x4a/0x58/0x71), plays animation with offset subtraction, calls reset_animation_state, transitions to state 2. State 2 continues animation via update_animation_timer. The -45 degree rotation likely tilts the creature to indicate being held/grabbed by player.

name: FUN_0040d360
summary: Main click/tap interaction response handler implementing a 4-state state machine. State 0: Initialization - plays oneshot click sound, stops current speech, transitions to state 1. State 1: Cursor detection - uses GetCursorPos and GetSystemMetrics to determine click quadrant (0-3), transitions to state 2. State 2: Direction setup - copies and normalizes direction vector (param_4[6/7/8]) from click point into this+0x194/0x198/0x19c storage, transitions to state 3. State 3: Main response - builds speech path triple (DAT_00459f30/"reaction_dekopin"/empty), triggers BIO speech via start_bio_speech_for_context, smoothly interpolates creature's orientation from current direction toward click direction using lerp timer at this+0x18c (increments by DAT_00452724, wraps at DAT_00452728), selects animation based on growth stage via param_4[0x11] floating-point selector (maps to animation IDs 8/0x17/0x2c/0x4a/0x58/0x71 with offsets 0/0xF/0x21/0x37/0x4E/0x60 subtracted for play_animation call), updates creature physics via update_position_with_physics, and transitions to state 0x36 (54) when animation completes. Response flag at this+0x1f8 prevents duplicate speech triggering.

name: FUN_0040e6b0
summary: Breeding and genetic inheritance handler implementing DNA transfer from 'yours' to 'mine' creature. States 0-1 (initialization/genetics): Checks this+0x1a8 for speed interpolation. Selects animation via growth selector - uses animation set 0/0x13/0x27/0x37/0x4e/0x60 (shared with directional_blend). Calls play_animation_with_growth_offset. On animation start, transitions to State 1 which handles genetics: generates two random bits via _rand()&0x80000001 for element selection (LSB extraction). First rand chooses Element01 or Element02 to READ from 'SeamanPC\Bio\DNA_Yours_Element01' or '_Element02' via get_variable_index_wrapper. If read value is 0 (not set), generates random DNA value via _rand()%14+1 (range 1-14). Second rand chooses Element01 or Element02 to WRITE to 'SeamanPC\Bio\DNA_Mine_Element01' or '_Element02' via set_index_wrapper. Zeros both DNA_Yours elements to clear parent contribution. Returns unique code 6 (breeding completion signal). Sets counter this+0x1b4=2, transitions to State 2. State 2 (animation loop): Plays looping animation with FUN_00414890. Decrements counter on each loop completion. When counter <1, transitions to State 3. State 3 (animation change): Selects new animation set - note uses 0x74 (116 decimal) for HG2 stage vs previous 0x60 (96), suggesting special post-breeding animation. Calls play_animation_with_growth_offset in forward non-looping mode. On animation ready, transitions to State 4. State 4 (waiting phase): Plays forward non-looping animation. Monitors param_5[0x13] (likely animation playback time or age counter) until exceeds threshold DAT_00452734. When threshold crossed, transitions to State 5. State 5 (exit): Plays forward non-looping animation. On animation complete (return 1), transitions to state 0x36. Updates physics throughout. Always returns 0 except State 1 returns 6.

name: FUN_0040f1a0
summary: Breeding initiation handler implementing copulation readiness verification and trigger for HG2 stage only. Entry guard: Checks global flag at DAT_004690a0+0x518 (likely breeding system enabled). If zero (disabled) OR param_4[0x11]!=1.12104e-44 (not HG2 stage), immediately transitions to state 0x36 and returns (no breeding). This restricts breeding to HG2 (final mature stage) only. State 0 (identity check): Reads 'SeamanPC\bio\MySelf' variable via get_variable_index_wrapper to determine self-identity. If value==0 (unset or primary creature), selects animation 0x79 (121 decimal - primary copulation animation). Else compares param_5 against global pointer at DAT_004690a0+0x540 (likely partner creature reference). If param_5 matches global (is partner), selects animation 0x7a (122 decimal - partner/secondary copulation animation). Zeros param_4[0x13] (likely copulation timer/counter reset). Calls play_animation_with_growth_offset with selected animation. On animation start, sets counter this+0x1b4=6 (copulation duration in cycles), transitions to state 1. State 1 (copulation loop): Plays looping animation with FUN_00414890. Decrements counter on each loop. When counter <1 (copulation duration elapsed), checks GetActionEvent for code 1. If event code 1 detected (likely completion trigger or interruption): clears 'SeamanPC\Action\Event' variable via set_index_wrapper, clears 'SeamanPC\Bio\IsReadyToCopulate' flag to 0 (marks breeding complete), transitions to state 0x36. Updates physics throughout.

name: FUN_0040fb30
summary: Camera-relative left turn behavior implementing view-oriented steering. State 0: Selects animation ID based on growth selector (1→0xc, 2→0x1b, 3→0x2e, GM5→0x39, HG2→0x4f/99). Calls play_animation_with_growth_offset, then retrieves camera orientation via vtable methods (+0x5c, +0x7c) on param_6. Performs three cross products on stored orientation matrix at this+0x168-0x17c to build perpendicular axis vectors (local_24/20/1c), computes yaw angle via fpatan(local_c, local_4) scaled by DAT_00452758 for degrees. Copies 9 floats from this+0x15c orientation to stack, calls compose_triple_rodrigues_extract_axis (FUN_0043cfc0) with three rotation axes and angles to produce final direction vector, writes to param_5[6/7/8]. Plays forward animation (FUN_00414890 mode 1, loop 0), updates physics. On animation complete (return 1), checks GetActionEvent - if code 1 detected, clears SeamanPC\Action\Event variable. Transitions to state 0x36. This implements strafe-left movement relative to camera view direction.

name: FUN_00410700
summary: Complex rotation stabilization with facial morph playback implementing recovery from tumble or spin. State 0 (this+0xd8==0): Checks this+0x1a8 for speed interpolation. Selects animation via growth selector (0xc/0x1b/0x2e/0x39/0x4f/99 - shares IDs with left turn handler). Calls play_animation_with_growth_and_timing (FUN_00414750) which validates timing vs basic play_animation. On success, retrieves camera orientation via vtable +0x5c/+0x7c on param_6, stores orientation components. Computes three cross products to extract perpendicular axes from orientation matrix at this+0x168-0x17c: this+0x15c=(this+0x17c*this+0x16c - this+0x178*this+0x170), this+0x160=(this+0x170*this+0x174 - this+0x17c*this+0x168), this+0x164=(this+0x178*this+0x168 - this+0x16c*this+0x174). Sets param_3+8 flag=1 to enable morph targets, transitions to state 1 (this+0xd8=1). State 1: Calls morph_target_playback_vtable (FUN_00417d90) on param_3 to animate facial expression during rotation - validates range param_3+0x20 vs +0x24, retrieves morph data from +0x2c array via multiple vtable methods. Computes additional cross product on local variables (local_24/20/1c = cross product of local vectors). Calculates yaw angle via fpatan(local_c, local_4) scaled by DAT_00452758 to degrees. Copies 9 floats from this+0x15c to stack, calls compose_triple_rodrigues_extract_axis (FUN_0043cfc0) with three rotation axes and angles (fVar8, fVar9, fVar10) to produce final direction, writes to param_5[6/7/8]. Plays animation with update_animation_timer_bidirectional (FUN_00414910 forward mode) for smooth windup. On complete (return 1): checks this+0x1ec trigger flag - if set, clears this+0xa8 main state, zeros flag, resets SeamanPC\Action\Event. Transitions to this+0xd4=0x36 (alternate state field). Manually calculates growth-offset-adjusted animation index via switch on param_5[0x11] subtracting offsets (0/15/33/55/78/96) from this+0x1b0, calls play_animation to get duration, stores at param_5[0x12]. Calls reset_animation_state, zeros param_5[0x13], sets this+0x1f0=1. Calls update_distance_and_movement_flags, sets this+0x1a8=1. Updates physics via update_position_with_physics.

name: FUN_00413530
summary: Screen-space jump/leap behavior trigger with dual-mode support. Checks busy flag at this+0xdc (returns 2 if active). Mode determined by param_2: if 0, uses provided position (param_5-7), if non-zero uses calculated screen-center offset. Position calculation queries screen height (GetSystemMetrics SM_CYSCREEN), divides by 2, adjusts ±25% based on MySelf variable (SeamanPC\bio\MySelf) for multi-creature scenarios, scales by _DAT_004526a8. Validates current animation state via get_field_at_offset_8 - rejects incompatible states (idle, eating, sleeping, panic, etc.). On success: sets target state 0x36 at +0xd4, clears +0xd8, sets +0x1a4 flag, plays leap sound (stop existing, play new), calls forward_to_subsystem_handler with destination vector. Returns 0 (initiated), 1 (already leaping), or 2 (busy/invalid).

name: FUN_00413780
summary: Conditional state handler that positions and triggers screen-dependent behavior based on creature state and identity. If this+0xdc==0 (unprocessed), reads screen width via GetSystemMetrics(SM_CXSCREEN), checks SeamanPC_bio_MySelf variable to select positioning constant (_DAT_00452780 vs _DAT_00452784, likely distinguishing player's creature from others), calculates X position as screen_width * constant. Retrieves subsystem state via get_field_at_offset_8 and switches: most states trigger action by setting this+0xd4=54, this+0xd8=0, this+0x1a4=1, calling forward_to_subsystem_handler with param 9 and position array (X, 0.0, 0.0, plus flags), returning 0. States 7/9 return 1 without action. States 0,1,4,11,19,38,41-44 return 2 (skip). Already-processed (this+0xdc!=0) returns 2 immediately.

name: FUN_004197b0
summary: Primary loader for Seaman's facial expression morph targets. Constructs filename from template 's_head1_face_BMP_0045acd0' with param_3 digit replacement (lines 181-187), builds full path from executable directory + '\Resource\' (DAT_0045aaec) + param_2 archive name (lines 191-257), opens archive via fopen_read_binary (line 258). Changes to temp directory via FUN_004183a0 (line 259), counts archive records via FUN_00418200 (line 260), extracts all files to temp via FUN_00418270 (line 261), closes archive (line 262). Processes extracted morphs: calls vtable method +0x5c on param_1 (line 265), creates object via vtable on unaff_ESI with &DAT_00452ea0 vtable (line 266), iterates and filters morphs via FUN_0041c370 at this+0x58 (line 270), cleans up temp files via FUN_00418360 (line 274). Restores original working directory (line 275) and frees directory buffer (line 276).

name: FUN_0041d580
summary: Complete Ogilby character sprite system constructor: sets vtable to PTR_LAB_004528a0, calls FUN_0041d830 to initialize action event state structure (zeros fields, writes SeamanPC\Action\Event variable), stores pointers to three pre-initialized global animation controllers at param_1+4 (DAT_00465298 move animation with 9 frames), param_1+8 (DAT_00465260 speak mouth with 2 frames), param_1+12 (DAT_00465350 home mouth), then initializes five numeric fields at offsets 0x30-0x40 with values 0x1b/0x1e/0x21/0x23/0 (likely frame indices or timing parameters). This aggregates the individual sprite components into unified character animation controller. The three animation slots (move/speak/mouth) allow concurrent body and facial animations.

name: FUN_0041d830
summary: Initializes action event structure: zeros 11 fields (param_1+4 through +0x44), sets param_1+0x18 to 0xFFFFFFFF, writes 0 to SeamanPC\Action\Event variable via FUN_004012c0 (set variable accessor). Reads global pointer from DAT_004690a0+0x53c.

name: FUN_00420a80
summary: Comprehensive object destructor implementing systematic resource teardown in specific order: (1) frees string buffer at +0xf4 and global buffer DAT_004653b0, (2) clears flag at +0x16, (3) iterates doubly-linked list from head (+0xd6) to sentinel (+0xe3), unlinking and freeing each node, (4) destroys polymorphic object at +0xf3 via vtable destructor (+8), (5) destroys graphics object at +0xd4 (same offset as FUN_0041f450), (6) calls cleanup_four_object_pointers for offsets 0x6c/0x68/0x278/0x27c, (7) invokes base class destructor with vtable reset to PTR_FUN_00452dc4. Exception-safe via SEH with state tracking. Likely destructor for major game entity (creature, player, NPC) with multiple subsystems (graphics, AI, behaviors).

name: FUN_00423dc0
summary: Core initialization for Seaman's multi-layer aquarium background sprite system. Validates Direct3D interfaces via three vtable checks (0x10, 0x18, 0x48) with MessageBoxA error dialogs, releases failed objects via vtable method 8. Creates 21×3 grid of texture handles (63 total) stored to param_1+0x3c8 array via vtable 0xa0 with scale factor _DAT_0045265c and Z-depth 0x40000000 (2.0). Processes 40 background layer objects starting at index 121 with step +3, calling vtable methods 0xa8/0x30/0x38 for texture setup and color manipulation.

name: FUN_00425d60
summary: Reads database variable 'SeamanPC\Bio\myself_ID' via ReadElementKey wrapper if DAT_004690a0+0x518 is non-zero. No-op if database not initialized.

name: FUN_00426bc0
summary: UI message polling function that walks linked list starting at param_1+0x354 (same list used by FUN_00426210 light propagation) via FUN_00427060 retrieving node pointers at offset -4, invokes vtable method at offset 0x28 on each node (likely 'has_pending_message' or 'needs_attention' query), continues iteration until method returns 0 (found pending component) or reaches sentinel at param_1+0x350, then calls FUN_00404770 on the stopped node which sets SeamanPC\Control\MessageFlag variable to 1 and invokes FUN_00434320 notification system passing global context DAT_004689fc and node ID at node+0x5c. This implements priority-based message dispatch where first component with pending state gets serviced. The early-break pattern ensures only one component processed per call.

name: FUN_00428490
summary: Background mail synchronization thread that orchestrates multiple services in sequence. First loads Preference.dll and calls GetMail function pointer (DAT_004654dc+0x140) with user paths stored in local buffers initialized from DAT_00465090. Updates new mail count via SetNewArriveMailNum, then either shows error messages (negative count) or loads inbox and mail log (positive count). Copies result paths to global structure at DAT_004654dc+0x1d4 and +0x214. Sets multiple status flags (0x1b8-0x1bc, 0x234) and conditionally performs: relation index database write if new contacts found (flag 0x1b9), seamail.tv server HTTP sync if enabled (flag 0x1c0 creates web object and calls connect_to_seamail_server), weather data fetch via WeatherGet.dll, and hourly mail time check via SeamanMail.dll if user preference flag 10 is set. Cleanup includes FreeLibrary on Preference.dll handle (offset 0x11c) and zero-clearing function pointers before terminating via exit_thread_with_cleanup.

name: FUN_00428da0
summary: Complete email sending worker thread implementation for the seamail.tv network infrastructure. Loads SeamanMail.dll from constructed path (DAT_00465090 base + offset 500 + 'SeamanMail.dll'), resolves NWsendmail function via GetProcAddress, calls it with 7 parameters extracted from mail configuration structure at DAT_004654dc (offsets +0x238 mode, +0x1d8/+0x1f8/+0x1e8/+0x208 string pointers likely to/from/subject/body, +0x14c function pointer storage). On success sets status flag at +0x1b7 to 1; on failure shows error via FUN_00429130 or calls error callback via FUN_00401540 if mode==1. Always exits via FUN_004430b0 thread cleanup with appropriate exit code.

name: FUN_0042ad50
summary: Implements conditional relationship database update logic for Seaman's social system. Performs case-insensitive string comparison via case_insensitive_string_compare_multibyte (FUN_0044df50) between this and param_1 against reference string DAT_00465090. If comparison returns 0 (strings match), creates new relationship entry by calling new_relation_wrapper (FUN_004014e0) with global context pointer (DAT_004654dc+0x114) and param_2. Otherwise, updates existing relationship via change_relation_wrapper (FUN_004014a0) using same context pointer, param_1, and param_2. The DAT_00465090 reference likely contains a default/initial relationship state string. Global pointer at DAT_004654dc+0x114 probably references the active relationship database or user context object.

name: FUN_00434040
summary: Global variable database initialization routine for Seaman's state management system. Zeros global flag DAT_00468a00 (likely 'initialized' or 'locked' flag). Stores param_1 pointer to critical global DAT_004689fc (the variable store base address - see MEMORY.md Phase 11, used by FUN_004012a0/c0 get/set accessors). Initializes param_1 structure by zeroing offsets 0x0, 0x8-0x14, 0x118-0x128, 0x18-0x1C, and 0x4. Sets param_1[0x47] to 0xFFFFFFFF (sentinel value at offset 0x11C = 284 decimal). Final zero at offset 0x4 overrides earlier zero at 0x0. Used during game startup or save file load to establish the runtime variable database that stores SeamanPC\Bio\* and User\* variables.

name: FUN_00434440
summary: Speech system event callback dispatcher implementing two event types. Loads event type from param_1+4 into iVar1 (line 2183). Calls translate_index_to_id to convert index to ID stored in DAT_004689fc global variable store, result overwrites param_1 (line 2184). Branches on event type at param_1+8 (line 2185): Type 0 (completion event, lines 2186-2189): calls lookup_handler_by_id to find handler for translated ID, if non-null calls SoundPlayEndEvent(handler) to signal playback completion. Type 1 (state update event, lines 2191-2195): writes three database variables using set_index_wrapper: 'SeamanPC\Action\Mouth' = param_1+0xc (mouth pose index), 'SeamanPC\Action\Face' = param_1+0x10 (facial expression index), 'SeamanPC\Action\Event' = param_1+0x14 (event trigger), then returns. Type validation ensures only 0 and 1 handled, other values ignored (line 2197).

name: FUN_004385a0
summary: Initializes Direct3D Retained Mode rendering device with error handling and quality configuration. First calls Direct3DRMCreate to get IDirect3DRM interface (stored in local_4), creates IDirect3DRMDevice at param_1+0x250 using GUID (DAT_00452e80), then releases the temporary interface. Configures device with quality options: sets render state 0x40 (likely shading mode) with value piVar3, and sets option 0x20 (possibly texture filtering or depth buffer). On failure at any stage, formats error message to global buffer DAT_00468f34 and displays MessageBox with 'Error_CSeamanDomain__CreateDirec' title. Returns 1 (success) or 0 (failure).

name: FUN_00439ed0
summary: Implements application suspension when entering screensaver mode. First calls deactivate_type15_object (FUN_00426b10) to search for and cleanup the type-0x15 object in the linked list at param_1+0x51c, performing data swaps and virtual method cleanup. Then, if valid API handle exists at param_1+0x53c (not -1), writes 0 to database variable SeamanPC_Api_f_screensaver via set_index_wrapper (FUN_004012c0) to signal idle/suspended state. Finally invokes callback chain via invoke_callback_chain_with_param (FUN_0043ba20) using different global pointers (DAT_004690ac/b4/bc/c4) than the exit handler, with first two callbacks receiving parameter from FUN_0043f660(). The screensaver variable transition (1→0 for enter, 0→1 for exit) controls power-saving behavior.

name: FUN_0043a210
summary: Death handling system that detects Seaman life state and offers resurrection via confirmation dialogs. First checks global flag DAT_00469094 (0=allow revive, 1=already revived/disabled). Calls get_myself_db_wrapper on Seaman database entity (this+0x53c) to get life state: values 1-2 indicate juvenile deaths (malnutrition, neglect), value 3 indicates adult death (old age, disease). For juveniles, displays simple confirmation (MessageBox 0x124 = Yes/No with icon). For adults, displays different message (different text at 0x00460a44 vs 0x004609d4) and on Yes (return 6), calls SeamanDeadToAlive resurrection function, sets global DAT_00469094 to 1 (prevent multiple revives), and resets hunger via handle_hunger_state with buffer DAT_00468e90. The tiered dialog system gives context-appropriate revival options based on death circumstances.

name: FUN_0043dae0
summary: Complete Windows application entry point handling all initialization phases: extracts module path, backs up error logs, validates minimum system requirements (800x600, DX7), enforces single-instance via "Seamail" mutex, initializes COM/OLE subsystems, verifies Active Desktop availability (prompts user to enable if disabled), creates hidden window, allocates 3240-byte main game object at DAT_004690a0, initializes game subsystems and graphics (with fullscreen fallback), loads input hook DLL (InputDLL_VC.dll) for mouse/keyboard capture, configures screensaver settings, runs Windows message loop, then performs comprehensive cleanup and optionally launches SeamanUpdate.exe updater on exit.

name: __mbsnbicoll
summary: Standard library function __mbsnbicoll implementing byte-count-limited, case-insensitive, locale-aware multibyte string comparison. Early-returns 0 if _MaxCount is 0 (empty comparison). Calls compare_string_locale_aware (FUN_0044f729) with parameters: codepage from DAT_00469c64, case-insensitive flag (1), both strings with _MaxCount byte limit, and locale from DAT_00469a48. Transforms Windows CompareString return values (1/2/3 for less/equal/greater) to standard strcmp convention: returns 0x7FFFFFFF (max int) on error (CompareString returned 0), otherwise returns (result-2) mapping to -1/0/+1. Used for case-insensitive sorting and matching of Japanese text in Seaman's dialogue and database systems.
